name: Ria Money Transfer
description_short: Ria Money Transfer is a global money transfer service operating through a network of agent locations offering cash pickup, bank deposit, and digital wallet options across 40+ countries.
description_long:
Ria Money Transfer operates as an international remittance and money transfer service with a distributed agent-based network rather than traditional brick-and-mortar branches. The company enables consumers to send and receive money globally through multiple channels and receive methods, positioning itself in the money services business rather than as a traditional financial institution.

The platform offers multiple ways to send money: online through their website, via mobile app, in person at agent locations, and through WhatsApp integration. Recipients can collect funds through bank deposits, cash pickup at agent locations, digital wallets, home delivery, or ATM withdrawals. The service covers six continental regions (Africa, Asia, Europe, Latin America, North America, and Oceania) with specific country coverage including major remittance corridors like Mexico, Philippines, and India.

Ria's distribution model relies heavily on retail partnerships and independent agents rather than owned locations. The website shows integration with convenience stores (7-Eleven), check-cashing services (PLS Check Cashers), and local multiservice businesses such as hair salons, grocery stores, pharmacies, and cargo services. This agent network approach allows rapid geographic expansion without capital-intensive branch infrastructure. They also offer ancillary services including bill payment processing and currency conversion tools.

While Ria provides convenient access points and multiple send/receive options, consumers should note the company's fees, exchange rates, and delivery timeframes are not disclosed on the location finder page. The agent-based model means service quality and hours may vary by location, and not all agents offer all services. Consumers should compare pricing with competitors before transferring larger amounts, as money transfer margins can be substantial.
pros: Multiple send methods including online, app, in-person, and WhatsApp for flexibility
Extensive global reach covering 40+ countries across six continents
Diverse receive options including bank deposit, cash pickup, digital wallet, home delivery, and ATM withdrawal
Wide agent network leveraging convenience stores (7-Eleven), check cashers, and local businesses for accessibility
24-hour availability at selected locations (7-Eleven and PLS Check Cashers partners)
Bill payment services available at many agent locations alongside money transfer
Mobile app with transfer tracking capabilities
cons: Fees, exchange rates, and delivery timeframes not transparent on website location finder
Service availability and hours vary significantly by agent location, some open as late as 10 AM or 11 AM
Not all agent locations offer all services (some locations limited to send-only or receive-only)
Dependent on third-party agent reliability and quality, which may be inconsistent
No pricing comparison tool visible on the location finder interface
best_for: International remittance senders with family or business connections in covered countries
Consumers seeking flexible send options (online, app, in-person, WhatsApp) based on preference
Users needing multiple receive methods including cash pickup and digital wallet deposits
Individuals in areas with dense agent networks (urban areas with 7-Eleven and local multiservice businesses)
diagnosis: Ria Money Transfer is best for consumers regularly sending money internationally who value flexible send/receive options and convenience through widespread agent locations. The primary caveat is lack of transparent pricing and fee disclosure on the website, requiring customers to check rates directly or contact agents before committing to transfers.
services: In-person money transfer at agent locations
Multi-regional send coverage (Africa, Asia, Europe, Latin America, North America, Oceania)
